title: aldama oblongifolia
intro: Aldama oblongifolia, also known as the Mexican tea or Spanish tea, is a perennial shrub native to Mexico that belongs to the family Rosaceae. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The leaves and flowers of Aldama oblongifolia can be used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions that offer health benefits including reduced fever, improved digestion, and relief from menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of Aldama oblongifolia can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
